items.title: Phase 5a — strategy_history snapshot on manual PATCH (What Changed misattribution fix)
items.notes: Done 2026-06-17 (PR #1262, merged to staging). First of three Phase 5 sub-PRs. The manual PATCH strategy-edit path (routes/keyword-strategy.ts) did NOT snapshot strategy_history (only the AI-regen writeKeywordStrategy did), so StrategyDiff ('What Changed') misattributed human edits to the last AI regeneration. Extracted the snapshot INSERT+prune-to-5 from keyword-strategy-persistence.ts into a reusable snapshotStrategyHistory(workspaceId, previousStrategy, priorArrays) helper (guards on prior generatedAt; must run inside db.transaction BEFORE the replaceAll/upsert clobber). Called it at the top of the PATCH applyPatch transaction (reads prior table-backed arrays + uses the PRIOR generatedAt). Behavior-preserving refactor of the regen path. Integration test: PATCH-then-/diff attributes the edit to the new boundary; no-blob PATCH records no snapshot (guard). Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-17-strategy-redesign-phase-5-signal-generation.md (decisions: cron conservative+kill-switch behind signal-auto-recompute flag; on-mutation always-enqueue).

Worker FM-2 + component tests. LESSON: adding to BACKGROUND_JOB_TYPES requires a lockstep entry in tests/helpers/background-job-test-matrix.ts (BACKGROUND_JOB_LIFECYCLE_MATRIX) or background-job-coverage-contract fails. Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-17-strategy-redesign-phase-5-signal-generation.md.

items.title: Phase 5c — Automated recompute: daily activity-gated cron + on-mutation enqueues (behind signal-auto-recompute)
items.notes: PR opening to staging. Third/last Phase 5 build sub-PR — dark-launched behind the new signal-auto-recompute feature flag (default OFF; server-read via isFeatureEnabled). OWNER DECISIONS: cron conservative+kill-switch (un-forced — skips workspaces already fresh <24h; hasRecentActivity(ws,2) tight window; enqueues the 5b INTELLIGENCE_RECOMPUTE job); on-mutation ALWAYS-enqueue (every trigger), bounded by hasActiveJob dedup + apiCache 15-min TTL. New shared enqueueIntelligenceRecompute(ws) (flag-gated + deduped) in intelligence-recompute-job.ts. New daily cron server/insight-recompute-cron.ts (modeled on intelligence-crons.ts: 24h interval + 10-min startup delay + unref + isRunning guard; start/stop wired into startup.ts startSchedulers + index.ts gracefulShutdown — symmetry). On-mutation hooks: queueKeywordStrategyPostUpdateFollowOns (covers strategy edit + publish + content/schema callers) + rank-tracking-scheduler after the rank snapshot. Flag added to FEATURE_FLAG_CATALOG + Strategy group; lifecycle test as-of bumped 2026-06-16→2026-06-17. After this merges: build work DONE — then the DEFERRED cumulative scaled-code-review over ALL redesign work, then flag-removal + legacyAnalysis deletion, then operator-keeper-override (P3). Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-17-strategy-redesign-phase-5-signal-generation.md.

items.title: Cannibalization triage — operator-selectable keeper (override the auto-derived canonical page)
items.notes: Phase 3 follow-on. Today CannibalizationTriage auto-derives the keeper page (item.canonicalPath if set, else best-ranking via keeperPathOf). Owner wants to OVERRIDE which competing page is the keeper (real example: keep a keyword on a services page, not the auto-picked insights page). Scope: a per-issue keeper picker; the chosen keeper must flow into (a) which pages show 'Fix in editor' (non-keepers = duplicates), and (b) the Send-to-client payload canonicalPath. KEY CONSTRAINT: the override MUST persist across strategy regeneration — cannibalization_issues is delete-then-reinsert (replaceAllCannibalizationIssues clobbers canonical_path), so the operator's keeper choice can't live on that row. Store it durably keyed by normalized keyword (mirror the 3b-i resolution pattern: a tracked record / small override table / preserved-metadata Map on the delete-reinsert), and have keeperPathOf prefer the operator override over the auto-derivation. Do after the current Strategy redesign batch. Owner expects further refinements to surface as Strategy gets exercised.

items.title: Feature stack — keyword admin cutover and legacy shell retirement
items.source: Parallel simplification explorer audit 2026-06-09 — Feature Stack Simplification
items.notes: Audit update 2026-06-10: this is no longer a table/data-model unification item. The backend read model is already canonical through the Keyword Command Center routes/hooks, and the remaining work is admin-surface cutover + authority cleanup. Current state: `seo-keywords` already flag-switches between `KeywordHub` and legacy `KeywordCommandCenter`, `seo-ranks` already redirects into the Hub when the flag is on, and both Hub + KCC read the same KCC summary/rows/detail APIs. Remaining live duplication: `KeywordHub` and `KeywordCommandCenter` both still own search/deep-link/drawer/bulk-action UI concerns, and standalone `RankTracker` is still a writable surface (add/remove/pin/snapshot), not measurement-only yet. Safe follow-up scope: remove duplicated Hub-local websocket invalidation in favor of the shared admin invalidation registry, align rank-tracking route/error-contract and action-metadata drift with the KCC/Hub contract, extract one shared rank-tracking mutation/invalidation authority used by Hub/RankTracker/Strategy, then retire the flag-off `KeywordCommandCenter` shell and writable standalone `RankTracker` behavior only after parity is confirmed. Preserve keyword lifecycle actions, rank history, snapshot capture, pinned trends, strategy feedback, View-in-Hub deep links, and local SEO segments. Tests/guardrails: `KeywordHub.test.tsx`, `KeywordCommandCenterDeepLink.test.tsx`, `RankTracker.test.tsx`, rank/KCC route tests, `route-fold-in-seo-ranks`, shared-cache coherence tests, and a contract that `RANK_TRACKING_UPDATED` invalidates Hub, latest/history ranks, strategy, and client rank reads. | Admin surface audit 2026-06-11 (§7): HARD PRECONDITION — port LocalSeoVisibilityPanel mode=keywords + market-setup drawer entry into the Hub before flipping (KCC is their only keyword-mode mount; KeywordCommandCenter.tsx:433-450). Flag-ON blockers + cutover sequencing: docs/audits/2026-06-11-admin-surface-remediation-plan.md §Wave 4. | W4 Phase A complete (fix/keyword-hub-cutover-a): panel+KPIs+summary error ported, drawer pin/outcome/replaced-by/history-chart, UX blockers (filter reset, keepPreviousData, mobile, empty states, bulk clearance), redirect search-preservation, view_rankings repoint. | DONE 2026-06-11 (Keyword Hub cutover, fix/keyword-hub-cutover-c stacked on Phase B). Phase B flipped keyword-hub default to true; Phase C stripped the legacy stack: deleted KeywordCommandCenter.tsx + KCC-only subcomponents (KeywordRow/VariantSubRow) + RankTracker.tsx and their tests; folded seo-ranks into the Hub (every seo-ranks literal removed from src/; transitional redirect retained). Server: removed the RankTracker-only DELETE /api/rank-tracking/:workspaceId/keywords/:query untrack endpoint (pin endpoint survives; Hub drawer uses it). Flag: keyword-hub retired from FEATURE_FLAGS/catalog/groups + migration 135 drops its overrides; surviving Keyword Hub group keys = keyword-universe-full, keyword-value-scoring. Prevention: pr-check rules `Retired Keyword Hub feature flag key used in flag API` + `Retired seo-ranks route literal in src` (the latter promoted from the route-fold-in-seo-ranks drift test). Docs: keyword-command-center.md -> keyword-hub.md; route-removal-checklist refreshed for registry-driven nav; deprecation-lifecycle entries added (KeywordCommandCenter, RankTracker, seo-ranks -> removed). NEEDS_CONTEXT carried forward: the server full-model path (buildKeywordCommandCenterModel + buildKeywordCommandCenterRowsViaModel, ~1,200 lines) was NOT deleted — it is LIVE via the Hub's `local_candidates` advanced filter (buildKeywordCommandCenterRows dispatches to it; only producer of buildLocalSeoKeywordCandidates). The `includeLocalSeoDetails` branch is the only genuinely-dead sub-path (always passed false). Migrating local_candidates to the skinny path is non-trivial (OOM-guarded) — tracked separately.

items.title: Feature stack — inbox deliverable cleanup and stale wrapper removal
items.source: Parallel simplification explorer audit 2026-06-09 — Feature Stack Simplification
items.notes: Audit update 2026-06-10: this is no longer a 'complete the spine' item. The canonical runtime surface is already `InboxTab` -> `UnifiedInbox` over `ClientDeliverable` + `GET /api/public/deliverables/:workspaceId`; the remaining work is cleanup around that canonical path. Current state: `InboxTab` is already a thin wrapper over `UnifiedInbox`, unified deliverables are the canonical client read model, and deliverable reads are assembled centrally from physical + projected sources. Remaining live seams are intentional hybrid write boundaries: projected copy/content reviews still mutate through their source routes, approval apply now uses canonical `POST /api/public/deliverables/:workspaceId/:id/apply` while delegating to the proven approval-batch apply service (legacy approvals URL remains deprecated compatibility), and schema-plan/client-action/source-specific writes remain source-authoritative with mirror sync. Progress 2026-06-13: deliverable realtime parity is closed for physical mirror families. Approval-batch and client-action mirrors already broadcast `DELIVERABLE_SENT`/`DELIVERABLE_UPDATED`; schema-plan send already broadcasts `DELIVERABLE_SENT` at the route/service seam while schema-plan sync/cancel now broadcasts `DELIVERABLE_UPDATED`; work-order and briefing mirrors now broadcast `DELIVERABLE_SENT` on first mirror and `DELIVERABLE_UPDATED` on re-mirror, with regression coverage. Remaining cleanup scope: shrink `InboxTab`/`ClientDashboard` to the real unified prop contract, update stale dark-launch/cutover comments and tests, and retire or isolate legacy approval/client-action normalizers that no longer have production consumers without removing compatibility routes that are still intentionally authoritative. Preserve Decisions/Reviews/Conversations routing, note-based conversations, inline single-action approval, bulk modal, per-item edits, apply-to-website, projected copy/content reviews, requests, and the work-order track lane. Tests/guardrails: unified inbox read route, deliverables route, projected review route coverage, decision-adapter coverage for the canonical deliverable path, `InboxTab`/`UnifiedInbox` component coverage, approval dual-write tests, edited item propagation, public approval broadcast tests, schema-plan/work-order/briefing broadcast tests, and deliverable websocket subscriber wiring. DONE 2026-06-13: canonical inbox cleanup PR tightened the ClientDashboard/InboxTab test fixture contract to the real UnifiedInbox prop surface, removed stale dark-launch/flag-era comments and test names, and marked the old client_action/approval_batch normalizers as compatibility adapters while preserving their label helpers and all compatibility write/read routes. This sprint propagates ONE consistent value score everywhere, turns it into dollars, surfaces the per-keyword GSC data already collected-but-discarded (striking-distance, momentum), and builds the client-facing layer — all additive, reusing signals already in hand, no new data collection.
sprints.notes: Sequenced by effort-to-value with dependencies (item titles tagged Wave 1–5). Wave 1 makes the score honest+legible+consistent (foundation, unblocks the client display); Wave 2 = dollars; Wave 3 = surface discarded GSC signals (one per-keyword join → 3 surfaces); Wave 4 = the client keyword display layer; Wave 5 = opportunistic/deeper. Each item runs the established brainstorm→spec→plan→phased-PR flow and is flag-gated where it changes ranking/output. Audit source: 2026-06-05 conversation (10-agent wiring audit + diverse-lens ideation). || 2026-06-05 ANTI-DUPLICATION PLACEMENT AUDIT (6-surface inventory): each item annotated with its canonical HOME + verdict (exists / fold-into / new). items.title: One value score everywhere — unify ranking across content-plan, titles/metas, briefs, and the client briefing

Four surfaces rank the SAME keywords four contradictory ways: Hub uses valueScore, content-plan uses legacy opportunityScore (keyword-recommendations.ts:159), the client briefing sorts by raw VOLUME buckets (briefing-client-projection.ts:81, demoting high-value low-volume gaps), titles/metas lead by strategy slot order. Make them all rank by the single Hub valueScore. HIGHEST-PRIORITY — fixes the client briefing sort, the exact surface the client keyword display sits on. Flag-gated where it changes ranking. || PLACEMENT [KEYSTONE — architecture decision, brainstorm first]: reconcile the new keyword-value-score vs the existing opportunity-value OV model (two value models today — Hub valueScore is a bare number; OV exposes components + is what client surfaces render). Decide ONE source of truth, then unify ranking across content-plan (keyword-recommendations.ts:159), titles/metas, briefs, client-briefing (fix the volume-bucket sort briefing-client-projection.ts:81), internal-linking. ALSO absorbs the only real need of the DROPPED kwv-client-wins-this-month: re-rank named rank-movement wins on InsightsBriefingPage so the most valuable named mover leads. || KEYSTONE FOUNDATION DONE (PR 1 + PR 2, 2026-06-05): one classifier (deriveValueIntent — shared by both layers; comparison-intent drift closed), layered contract documented (Layer 1 = computeKeywordValueScore / Layer 2 = computeOpportunityValue; surface ownership rule), and KeywordValueComponents interface exposed via computeKeywordValueComponents (commercialValue/demand/winnability/localMultiplier/intent). items.title: API documentation (OpenAPI/Swagger spec)
items.source: Market prep analysis Mar 2026; revised May 2026 post-review
items.notes: 91 route files in server/routes/ with no formal API spec. Triggers to pull forward: (a) first external API consumer signed (paid agency white-label, Zapier, contractor onboarding), (b) committing to migrate response validation to Zod for other reasons (OpenAPI becomes a free byproduct), (c) hitting 'I don't know what this endpoint returns' friction during builds. Implementation: use @asteasolutions/zod-to-openapi — NOT tsoa (requires controller-class refactor incompatible with Express+middleware style) or swagger-jsdoc (hand-maintained doc blocks rot immediately). Prerequisites before start: (1) response schemas must be migrated from shared/types/ TS interfaces to Zod (currently only request schemas are Zod via validate() middleware) — this is the bulk of the work, not the spec emission, (2) per-route security tagging for the dual auth model (HMAC x-auth-token admin vs JWT client vs public token), (3) pr-check rule asserting every Express route has a registered OpenAPI entry to prevent drift (zod-to-openapi does NOT introspect the route table — registration is opt-in per route). Out of scope: WebSocket broadcasts (would need separate AsyncAPI spec). Deliverables: registered request+response schemas per route, /api/docs Swagger UI gated to authenticated admins only, generated spec in CI artifact, drift-detection test.

sprints.name: Intelligence Quality Consolidation — Recommendations & AI Content
sprints.rationale: Close the root quality gaps in platform insights and AI content generation by joining mature subsystems through the existing intelligence conventions instead of hand-rolling context, scoring, narrative, and evidence handling per feature. The target architecture is: workspace data -> intelligence slices -> shared generation context -> AI operation contract -> deterministic recommendation/story output -> tracked action/outcome -> future intelligence. The remaining keyword work extends this consolidation into a full keyword operating loop: provider source data -> shared keyword intelligence -> strategy/recommendation judgment -> rank-tracking propagation -> content/action follow-through -> outcome feedback.

items.title: Per-market keyword relevance — keyword↔market assignment model
items.notes: Today the refresh planner pairs every selected keyword with every active market — `dentist at houston` gets queried against Austin's market and surfaces Houston competitors as 'top local results' in the Austin context. Confirmed on Swish staging refresh 2026-05-21 (55 of 200 snapshots = 27.5% cross-market noise). Fix is non-trivial because it requires a keyword↔market assignment model. (1) New `keyword_markets` join table mapping which keywords should be checked against which markets. (2) Default: keyword applies to all active markets (matches current implicit behavior, no migration of existing data needed). (3) Override path: admin pins a keyword to specific market(s). (4) Auto-inference: if keyword text contains an active market's city/region name, default assignment to that market only. (5) Iterator + refresh planner respect the assignment. (6) UI: per-market detail blocks in keyword drawer instead of single block, with pin/unpin controls. (7) **Per-market volume/clicks/impressions reporting**: the keyword detail surface should show separate volume, GSC clicks, GSC impressions, and CTR per active market the keyword applies to — today these are workspace-global and don't distinguish between Austin and Houston performance for the same keyword. Requires per-market metric storage AND geo-targeted DataForSEO volume queries (see intel-quality-keyword-volume-geo-targeting). Deferred to P2 because no current client uses multi-market in production — Swish is the only multi-market workspace and is internal.

items.title: Schema engagement signals — wordCount, timeRequired, speakable, educationalLevel
items.notes: Article.wordCount shipped in the Blog/Article Schema Quality Hardening pass (May 2026) using visible content extraction instead of full articleBody duplication. Remaining quick-win Tier-4 fields with data the platform already has: Article.timeRequired = wordCount/250wpm rounded; WebPage.speakable with cssSelector pointing to .lead/.intro/first-paragraph — voice-assistant rich result; Article.educationalLevel via AI classification (Beginner/Intermediate/Advanced). All wire through existing PageData typed boundary. No new admin UI required for the basics; speakable cssSelector is a per-template constant. Independent rich-result eligibility unlock for Google Article + voice-search amplification.

items.title: Schema E-E-A-T amplifiers — publishingPrinciples, reviewedBy, copyrightHolder, isAccessibleForFree, Person.jobTitle
items.notes: Tier-3 trust signals Google increasingly weighs. Add 4 new optional Workspace fields: publishingPrinciplesUrl, actionableFeedbackPolicyUrl (both → Organization.publishingPrinciples + actionableFeedbackPolicy on every page's @graph). Article.copyrightHolder ref to Organization, copyrightYear from datePublished. Article.reviewedBy as Person from new CMS field 'fact_checker' (optional). Article.isAccessibleForFree from per-tier content gating. Person.jobTitle + worksFor on author when CMS author records have those fields (currently they're just string names). Mostly admin Settings UI work + template emission. Each field is an additive E-E-A-T signal — non-breaking, immediate ranking benefit on YMYL content.

items.title: Schema commerce types — Service.offers (PriceSpecification), Product, Course, Event
items.notes: Tier-5 monetization opportunity for agencies with productized services / paid content. Service.offers with Offer { price, priceCurrency, availability } — Google's 'service prices' SERP feature. Product schema for templates/digital products. Course for educational content/training. Event for webinars/launches with eventStatus + eventAttendanceMode (Online/Offline/Mixed). Each is independent rich-result eligibility unlock. Workspace.contentPricing already exists (used for content-purchase tiers); Service.offers can reuse the pricing infra. Per-type effort similar to parity-fields PR1's per-template additions; parallelizable across template files. Default-off; admin opts in per-workspace. Most useful for agencies with packaged services (web-design + brand audit + content retainer); skip for service-only sites without published prices.

items.title: Schema trust/authority — Organization.award, member, hasCredential, numberOfEmployees
items.notes: Tier-6 reputation graph fields. New Workspace.organizationAwards (string[] of Award names → Award entity[]), Workspace.organizationMemberships (industry orgs → ProgramMembership/Organization references), Workspace.organizationCredentials (certifications → EducationalOccupationalCredential), Workspace.numberOfEmployees (QuantitativeValue { minValue, maxValue, unitText: 'employee' }). Each emitted on Organization sibling node in homepage + local-business templates. Pure admin Settings UI + template extension; no AI/intelligence dependencies. Agency credibility in SERP, especially for B2B competitive queries.

items.title: Schema Intelligence Layer (FAQ-from-GSC, E-E-A-T, healthcare subtypes)

(2) FAQ enrichment from GSC question queries via a new slice-backed helper if this feature is revived; the old unused extractFaqOpportunities/schema prompt block was removed in Workspace Intelligence Consolidation PR4. (3) E-E-A-T author injection from content brief evidence into Article.author with Person.knowsAbout/sameAs. (4) Service serviceType from voice profile + business profile. Any revived intelligence enrichment must flow through buildSchemaIntelligence() or another documented central intelligence builder, not direct raw workspace reads.

sprints.name: ✅ SHIPPED — 📖 Client Dashboard Quick Wins 2 — Tell the Story
sprints.rationale: Second quick-win wave from the 2026-06 client dashboard audit: narrative, freshness, and feedback-loop polish. The audit's core theme is that clients see raw results without interpretation or confirmation — these items add the missing 'what this means / we heard you' layer without restructuring any tab.
sprints.notes: Source: docs/audits/client-dashboard-2026-06/01-quick-wins.md. Several items touch the same components — sequence within the sprint to avoid merge churn. assembledAt (server half) ships with freshness stamps (client half) in one PR per the typed-contract data-flow rule.

items.title: assembledAt on intelligence payload + 'Data as of …' freshness stamps on metric surfaces
items.notes: Shipped in PR 1. Server half already existed via ClientIntelligence.assembledAt; this PR adds a shared FreshnessStamp primitive, real React Query dataUpdatedAt plumbing for client GA4/GSC hooks, visible stamps on Search/Analytics/Overview metric surfaces, and removes the synthetic client-intelligence fallback timestamp so failed intelligence reads cannot render as fresh.

items.title: Analytics sub-tab: deterministic takeaway summary + conversion-rate color coding
items.notes: Shipped in PR 2. AnalyticsTab now renders a deterministic, template-based takeaway from GA4 traffic, top source/page, and top tracked action with no AI call. Conversion rates remain display percentages and use judgment colors only: emerald for 5%+, amber for 2-4.9%, red below 2%, while raw conversion counts remain neutral metric text.

items.title: ROI methodology explainer ('How we calculate this')
items.notes: Shipped in PR 4. ROIDashboard now includes an expandable 'How we calculate this' explainer that describes organic traffic value, ad spend equivalent, revenue-at-stake/content attribution, and conservative assumptions without changing ROI formulas or adding route reads.

items.title: Composite health ring breakdown (churn 40 / ROI 30 / engagement 30)
items.notes: Shipped in PR 5. Client intelligence now projects a sanitized composite health breakdown for Growth+ workspaces, and the existing HealthScoreCard renders an expandable component view for retention signals, ROI momentum, and portal engagement without exposing raw churnRisk or adding route reads.

items.title: Post-approval 'next step' toast in Inbox
items.notes: Shipped in PR 3. Successful unified-inbox approve responses now show a next-step toast: "Approved. We're publishing this. Track it in your inbox." This covers both standard DecisionCard approval and inline approval-family cards while preserving held-item, declined, feedback, and apply-to-website flows.

items.title: Flag-submission confirmation + optimistic flagged-state on Content Plan cells
items.notes: Shipped in PR 3. Content Plan cell flagging now updates the visible cell to flagged immediately with the submitted note, reconciles from the server after success, rolls back on API failure, and shows success/error toasts so the tab confirms the client was heard.

items.title: Comment-count badges on work-order conversation threads
items.notes: Shipped in PR 6. Work-order deliverables now serialize a list-side commentCount from a single batched work_order_comments aggregate, including 0 for empty threads, and the client Work in progress cards render a blue comment-count Badge for 0/1/plural conversation counts without using per-thread reads for the badge.

items.title: Keyword feedback summary card on Strategy ('You approved 91% of suggestions')
items.notes: Shipped in PR 7. Growth+ client intelligence now projects a sanitized keywordFeedbackSummary from ClientSignalsSlice, and Strategy renders a blue data SectionCard with approve/reject counts, approval rate, display samples, and top rejection reasons.

items.title: 'We Called It' prediction card narrative expansion
items.notes: Shipped in PR 7. PredictionShowcaseCard now renders each stored outcome as a Before/After story with the recorded score badge, page, and confirmation date, using existing WeCalledItEntry fields only.

items.title: Inline prices on every purchase CTA before Stripe redirect
items.notes: Shipped in PR 8. Content and Inbox submit-request service choosers now show brief/full-post prices and selected CTA price before checkout, PlansTab upgrade/subscription CTAs include monthly prices, and external billing workspaces hide platform prices while billing actions route to contact/chat instead of Stripe; content subscription checkout also has a server-side external-billing guard.
